revision.comment: 「小幡長塚古墳」立ち上げました。修正・補足などあれば宜しく。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=小幡長塚古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション=小幡長塚古墳 {{国土航空写真}} |所在地=愛知県名古屋市守山区小幡4丁目地内 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E|}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=墳長約81m、前方部幅約42m、後円部径約38m |築造年代=6世紀前半 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=須恵器、形象埴輪 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''小幡長塚古墳'''（おばたながつかこふん）は[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]にある[[古墳]]。地元では単に'''長塚古墳'''と呼ぶ。
==概要== 小幡ヶ原周辺に点在する古墳の一つで[[前方後円墳]]。周辺は明治時代後半から[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]]の小幡ヶ原射撃場として使用され、墳丘には演習の際に掘られた[[塹壕]]の痕跡が現在も残る。かつて墳丘は二重周濠に囲まれていたが、戦後の開発において周堤は取り壊され、現在では墳丘の側まで住宅が密集している。なお、墳丘自体は名古屋市の管理する緑地「喜多山公園」として保存されている。
[[名古屋市教育委員会]]による[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）以降の墳丘・周濠の発掘調査によって、[[須恵器]]や[[埴輪]]の破片が多数見つかっており、墳丘や出土品の形態などから6世紀前半に築造されたと考えられている。
なお、地元住民によれば、かつては複数の[[陪塚]]があったことからこの周辺は'''七塚'''と呼ばれていたともいうが、現存せず記録も残されていないため塚の規模や数については不明である。
==規模と構造== 主軸は北西30.5°で、推定される規模は墳丘長約81メートル、前方部の幅約42メートル、高さが5.5メートル、後円部径約38メートル、高さが6.5メートル。先に記したように墳丘の近くまで住宅があるため内濠を含めた墳裾部の詳細な調査は行なわれておらず、墳丘の規模については必ずしも確定した数値ではない。近年の調査では墳丘長約74メートル、前方部幅約48メートル、後円部径約42メートルの数値も挙げられている。また、2段築造であったと推定されており、くびれ部北東側に造り出しの痕跡らしきものがみられる。墳丘の発掘調査は行なわれておらず内部主体は不明。葺石は確認されていない。
[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）の愛知県の記録によれば墳丘は盾型の二重周濠に囲まれており、外堤の高さは180cmほどあった。[[愛知県道59号名古屋第二環状線]]（茶臼山交差点〜小幡ヶ原交差点間）建設に伴う[[2008年]]度の調査では前方部北側の中堤の角が検出され、先の発掘調査の結果と重ね合わせると、前方部外濠の幅は18〜19メートルに及び、周濠を含めた古墳の全長135〜140メートル、外濠前面幅約125メートルと推定されている。
==出土品== *須恵器（高坏、甕） *円筒埴輪 *朝顔型埴輪 *人物埴輪（腕・手の破片） *家型埴輪 *動物埴輪（水鳥など）
くびれ部に近い外濠の中堤近辺から多数が出土しているがほとんどが破片で、出土状況などから後年になって意図的に破壊された可能性もあるとしている。また、[[三八式歩兵銃]]のものとみられる弾丸なども出土しており、出土品は[[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]に収蔵されている。
==参考文献== <small> *『長塚古墳発掘調査報告書』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2006年（平成19年） *『埋蔵文化財調査報告書 55』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2007年（平成20年） *『長塚古墳第3次発掘調査現地説明会資料』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館、2009年（平成22年） </small>
==アクセス== *[[名鉄瀬戸線]] - [[喜多山駅 (愛知県)|喜多山駅]]から徒歩7分 *[[名古屋市営バス]] -「喜多山」バス停から徒歩8分
==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} *[http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html 名古屋を歩こう - 守山区 小幡]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはたなかつかこふん}} [[Category:守山区]] [[Category:愛知県の古墳]]

revision.comment: 微修正・追記。外部リンク追加。

==アクセス== *[[名鉄瀬戸線]] - [[喜多山駅 (愛知県)|喜多山駅]]から徒歩7分 *[[名古屋市営バス]] -「喜多山」バス停から徒歩8分
==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} *[http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html 名古屋を歩こう - 守山区 小幡] *[http://archive.gsi.go.jp/airphoto/ 国土地理院 国土変遷アーカイブ] **[http://archive.gsi.go.jp/airphoto/ViewPhotoServlet?workname=USA&courseno=R1595&photono=39 USA-R1595-39] - 1948年7月の米軍による空中写真、上端近くの中央左側に二重周濠があった当時の古墳が見られる。
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはたなかつかこふん}} [[Category:守山区]] [[Category:愛知県の古墳]]

なお、地元住民によれば、かつては複数の[[陪塚]]があったことからこの周辺は'''七塚'''と呼ばれていたともいうが、現存せず記録も残されていないため塚の規模や数については不明である。

[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）の愛知県の測量記録によれば墳丘は盾型の二重周濠に囲まれており、外堤の高さは180センチメートルほどであった。[[2006年]]（平成18年）の調査で北側の外堤に方形張出部が存在した可能性が指摘されている。また、[[愛知県道59号名古屋第二環状線]]（茶臼山交差点〜小幡ヶ原交差点間）建設に伴う[[2008年]]（平成20年）度の調査では前方部北側の中堤の角が検出され、先の発掘調査の結果と重ね合わせると、前方部外濠の幅は18〜19メートルに及び、周濠を含めた古墳の全長135〜140メートル、外濠前面幅約125メートルと推定されている。
==出土品== *須恵器（高坏、甕） *円筒埴輪 *朝顔型埴輪 *人物埴輪（腕・手の破片） *家型埴輪 *動物埴輪（水鳥など）
くびれ部に近い外濠の中堤近辺から多数が出土しているがほとんどが破片で、出土状況などから後年になって意図的に破壊された可能性もあるとしている。人物埴輪の出土は名古屋市内の古墳では初。また、[[三八式歩兵銃]]のものとみられる弾丸なども出土しており、出土品は[[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]に収蔵されている。
==参考文献== <small> *『長塚古墳発掘調査報告書』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2006年（平成19年） *『埋蔵文化財調査報告書 55』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2007年（平成20年） *『長塚古墳第3次発掘調査現地説明会資料』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館、2009年（平成22年） </small>
==アクセス== *[[名鉄瀬戸線]] - [[喜多山駅 (愛知県)|喜多山駅]]から徒歩7分 *[[名古屋市営バス]] -「喜多山」バス停から徒歩8分
==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} *[http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html 名古屋を歩こう - 守山区 小幡] *[http://archive.gsi.go.jp/airphoto/ 国土地理院 国土変遷アーカイブ] **[http://archive.gsi.go.jp/airphoto/ViewPhotoServlet?workname=USA&courseno=R1595&photono=39 USA-R1595-39] - 1948年7月の米軍による空中写真、上端近くの中央左側に二重周濠があった当時の古墳が見られる。
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはたなかつかこふん}} [[Category:守山区]] [[Category:愛知県の古墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=小幡長塚古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション=小幡長塚古墳 {{国土航空写真}} |所在地=愛知県名古屋市守山区小幡4丁目地内 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E|}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=墳長約81m、前方部幅約42m、後円部径約38m |築造年代=6世紀前半 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=須恵器、形象埴輪 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''小幡長塚古墳'''（おばたながつかこふん）は[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]にある[[古墳]]。地元では単に'''長塚古墳'''と呼ぶ。
==概要== 小幡ヶ原周辺に点在する古墳の一つで[[前方後円墳]]。周辺は明治時代後半から[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]]の小幡ヶ原射撃場として使用され、墳丘には演習の際に掘られた[[塹壕]]の痕跡が現在も残る。かつて墳丘は二重周濠に囲まれていたが、戦後の開発において周堤は取り壊され、周濠は全て埋め立てられた。現在では墳丘の側まで住宅が密集している。なお、墳丘自体は名古屋市の管理する緑地「喜多山公園」として保存されている。
[[名古屋市教育委員会]]による[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）以降の墳丘・周濠の調査によって、[[須恵器]]や[[埴輪]]の破片が多数見つかっており、墳丘や出土品の形態などから[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたと考えられている。
なお、地元住民によれば、かつては複数の[[陪塚]]があったことからこの周辺は'''七塚'''と呼ばれていたともいうが、現存せず記録も残されていないため塚の規模や数については不明である。
==規模と構造== 主軸は北西30.5°で、墳丘長約81メートル、前方部の幅約42メートル（推定）、高さが5.5メートル、後円部径約38メートル（推定）、高さが6.5メートル。先に記したように墳丘の近くまで住宅があるため内濠を含めた墳裾部の詳細な調査は行なわれておらず、墳丘の規模については必ずしも確定した数値ではない。近年の調査では墳丘長約74メートル、前方部幅約48メートル、後円部径約42メートルの数値も挙げられている。また、2段築造であったと推定されており、くびれ部北東側に造り出しの痕跡らしきものがみられる。墳丘の[[発掘調査]]は行なわれておらず内部主体は不明。[[葺石]]は確認されていない。
[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）の愛知県の測量記録によれば墳丘は盾型の二重周濠に囲まれており、外堤の高さは180センチメートルほどであった。[[2006年]]（平成18年）の調査で北側の外堤に方形張出部が存在した可能性が指摘されている。また、[[愛知県道59号名古屋第二環状線]]（茶臼山交差点〜小幡ヶ原交差点間）建設に伴う[[2008年]]（平成20年）度の調査では前方部北側の中堤の角が検出され、先の発掘調査の結果と重ね合わせると、前方部外濠の幅は18〜19メートルに及び、周濠を含めた古墳の全長135〜140メートル、外濠前面幅約125メートルと推定されている。
==出土品== *須恵器（高坏、甕） *円筒埴輪 *朝顔型埴輪 *人物埴輪（腕・手の破片） *家型埴輪 *動物埴輪（水鳥など）
くびれ部に近い外濠の中堤近辺から多数が出土しているがほとんどが破片で、出土状況などから後年になって意図的に破壊された可能性もあるとしている。人物埴輪の出土は名古屋市内の古墳では初。また、[[三八式歩兵銃]]のものとみられる弾丸なども出土しており、出土品は[[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]に収蔵されている。
==参考文献== <small> *『長塚古墳発掘調査報告書』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2006年（平成19年） *『埋蔵文化財調査報告書 55』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2007年（平成20年） *『長塚古墳第3次発掘調査現地説明会資料』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館、2009年（平成22年） </small>
==アクセス== *[[名鉄瀬戸線]] - [[喜多山駅 (愛知県)|喜多山駅]]から徒歩7分 *[[名古屋市営バス]] -「喜多山」バス停から徒歩8分
==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} *[http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html 名古屋を歩こう - 守山区 小幡] *[http://archive.gsi.go.jp/airphoto/ 国土地理院 国土変遷アーカイブ] **[http://archive.gsi.go.jp/airphoto/ViewPhotoServlet?workname=USA&courseno=R1595&photono=39 USA-R1595-39] - 1948年7月の米軍による空中写真、上端近くの中央左側に二重周濠があった当時の古墳が見られる。

くびれ部に近い外濠の中堤近辺から多数が出土しているがほとんどが破片で、出土状況などから後年になって意図的に破壊された可能性もあるとしている。人物埴輪の出土は名古屋市内の古墳では初。また、[[三八式歩兵銃]]のものとみられる弾丸なども出土しており、出土品は[[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]に収蔵されている。
==参考文献== <small> *『長塚古墳発掘調査報告書』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2006年（平成19年） *『埋蔵文化財調査報告書 55』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2007年（平成20年） *『長塚古墳第3次発掘調査現地説明会資料』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館、2009年（平成22年） </small>
==アクセス== *[[名鉄瀬戸線]] - [[喜多山駅 (愛知県)|喜多山駅]]から徒歩7分 *[[名古屋市営バス]] -「喜多山」バス停から徒歩8分
==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html 名古屋を歩こう - 守山区 小幡] * [[国土地理院]] [http://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do 地図・空中写真閲覧サービス] ** [http://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595-39] - 1948年7月の米軍による[[空中写真]]、上端近くの中央左側に二重周濠があった当時の古墳が見られる。
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはたなかつかこふん}} [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:愛知県の古墳]]

revision.comment: /* 外部リンク */ [[Category:前方後円墳]]追加。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=小幡長塚古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション=小幡長塚古墳 {{国土航空写真}} |所在地=愛知県名古屋市守山区小幡4丁目地内 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E|}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=墳長約81m、前方部幅約42m、後円部径約38m |築造年代=6世紀前半 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=須恵器、形象埴輪 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''小幡長塚古墳'''（おばたながつかこふん）は[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]にある[[古墳]]。地元では単に'''長塚古墳'''と呼ぶ。
==概要== 小幡ヶ原周辺に点在する古墳の一つで[[前方後円墳]]。周辺は明治時代後半から[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]]の小幡ヶ原射撃場として使用され、墳丘には演習の際に掘られた[[塹壕]]の痕跡が現在も残る。かつて墳丘は二重周濠に囲まれていたが、戦後の開発において周堤は取り壊され、周濠は全て埋め立てられた。現在では墳丘の側まで住宅が密集している。なお、墳丘自体は名古屋市の管理する緑地「喜多山公園」として保存されている。
[[名古屋市教育委員会]]による[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）以降の墳丘・周濠の調査によって、[[須恵器]]や[[埴輪]]の破片が多数見つかっており、墳丘や出土品の形態などから[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたと考えられている。
なお、地元住民によれば、かつては複数の[[陪塚]]があったことからこの周辺は'''七塚'''と呼ばれていたともいうが、現存せず記録も残されていないため塚の規模や数については不明である。
==規模と構造== 主軸は北西30.5°で、墳丘長約81メートル、前方部の幅約42メートル（推定）、高さが5.5メートル、後円部径約38メートル（推定）、高さが6.5メートル。先に記したように墳丘の近くまで住宅があるため内濠を含めた墳裾部の詳細な調査は行なわれておらず、墳丘の規模については必ずしも確定した数値ではない。近年の調査では墳丘長約74メートル、前方部幅約48メートル、後円部径約42メートルの数値も挙げられている。また、2段築造であったと推定されており、くびれ部北東側に[[造り出し]]の痕跡らしきものがみられる。墳丘の[[発掘調査]]は行なわれておらず内部主体は不明。[[葺石]]は確認されていない。
[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）の愛知県の測量記録によれば墳丘は盾型の二重周濠に囲まれており、外堤の高さは180センチメートルほどであった。[[2006年]]（平成18年）の調査で北側の外堤に方形張出部が存在した可能性が指摘されている。また、[[愛知県道59号名古屋第二環状線]]（茶臼山交差点〜小幡ヶ原交差点間）建設に伴う[[2008年]]（平成20年）度の調査では前方部北側の中堤の角が検出され、先の発掘調査の結果と重ね合わせると、前方部外濠の幅は18〜19メートルに及び、周濠を含めた古墳の全長135〜140メートル、外濠前面幅約125メートルと推定されている。
==出土品== *須恵器（高坏、甕） *円筒埴輪 *朝顔型埴輪 *人物埴輪（腕・手の破片） *家型埴輪 *動物埴輪（水鳥など）
くびれ部に近い外濠の中堤近辺から多数が出土しているがほとんどが破片で、出土状況などから後年になって意図的に破壊された可能性もあるとしている。人物埴輪の出土は名古屋市内の古墳では初。また、[[三八式歩兵銃]]のものとみられる弾丸なども出土しており、出土品は[[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]に収蔵されている。
==参考文献== <small> *『長塚古墳発掘調査報告書』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2006年（平成19年） *『埋蔵文化財調査報告書 55』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2007年（平成20年） *『長塚古墳第3次発掘調査現地説明会資料』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館、2009年（平成22年） </small>
==アクセス== *[[名鉄瀬戸線]] - [[喜多山駅 (愛知県)|喜多山駅]]から徒歩7分 *[[名古屋市営バス]] -「喜多山」バス停から徒歩8分
==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html 名古屋を歩こう - 守山区 小幡] * [[国土地理院]] [http://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do 地図・空中写真閲覧サービス] ** [http://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595-39] - 1948年7月の米軍による[[空中写真]]、上端近くの中央左側に二重周濠があった当時の古墳が見られる。
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはたなかつかこふん}} [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:愛知県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.comment: [[Category:愛知県の古墳]]を除去; [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]]を追加 ([[WP:HotCat|HotCat]]使用)
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=小幡長塚古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション=小幡長塚古墳 {{国土航空写真}} |所在地=愛知県名古屋市守山区小幡4丁目地内 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E|}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=墳長約81m、前方部幅約42m、後円部径約38m |築造年代=6世紀前半 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=須恵器、形象埴輪 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''小幡長塚古墳'''（おばたながつかこふん）は[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]にある[[古墳]]。地元では単に'''長塚古墳'''と呼ぶ。
==概要== 小幡ヶ原周辺に点在する古墳の一つで[[前方後円墳]]。周辺は明治時代後半から[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]]の小幡ヶ原射撃場として使用され、墳丘には演習の際に掘られた[[塹壕]]の痕跡が現在も残る。かつて墳丘は二重周濠に囲まれていたが、戦後の開発において周堤は取り壊され、周濠は全て埋め立てられた。現在では墳丘の側まで住宅が密集している。なお、墳丘自体は名古屋市の管理する緑地「喜多山公園」として保存されている。
[[名古屋市教育委員会]]による[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）以降の墳丘・周濠の調査によって、[[須恵器]]や[[埴輪]]の破片が多数見つかっており、墳丘や出土品の形態などから[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたと考えられている。
なお、地元住民によれば、かつては複数の[[陪塚]]があったことからこの周辺は'''七塚'''と呼ばれていたともいうが、現存せず記録も残されていないため塚の規模や数については不明である。
==規模と構造== 主軸は北西30.5°で、墳丘長約81メートル、前方部の幅約42メートル（推定）、高さが5.5メートル、後円部径約38メートル（推定）、高さが6.5メートル。先に記したように墳丘の近くまで住宅があるため内濠を含めた墳裾部の詳細な調査は行なわれておらず、墳丘の規模については必ずしも確定した数値ではない。近年の調査では墳丘長約74メートル、前方部幅約48メートル、後円部径約42メートルの数値も挙げられている。また、2段築造であったと推定されており、くびれ部北東側に[[造り出し]]の痕跡らしきものがみられる。墳丘の[[発掘調査]]は行なわれておらず内部主体は不明。[[葺石]]は確認されていない。
[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）の愛知県の測量記録によれば墳丘は盾型の二重周濠に囲まれており、外堤の高さは180センチメートルほどであった。[[2006年]]（平成18年）の調査で北側の外堤に方形張出部が存在した可能性が指摘されている。また、[[愛知県道59号名古屋第二環状線]]（茶臼山交差点〜小幡ヶ原交差点間）建設に伴う[[2008年]]（平成20年）度の調査では前方部北側の中堤の角が検出され、先の発掘調査の結果と重ね合わせると、前方部外濠の幅は18〜19メートルに及び、周濠を含めた古墳の全長135〜140メートル、外濠前面幅約125メートルと推定されている。
==出土品== *須恵器（高坏、甕） *円筒埴輪 *朝顔型埴輪 *人物埴輪（腕・手の破片） *家型埴輪 *動物埴輪（水鳥など）
くびれ部に近い外濠の中堤近辺から多数が出土しているがほとんどが破片で、出土状況などから後年になって意図的に破壊された可能性もあるとしている。人物埴輪の出土は名古屋市内の古墳では初。また、[[三八式歩兵銃]]のものとみられる弾丸なども出土しており、出土品は[[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]に収蔵されている。
==参考文献== <small> *『長塚古墳発掘調査報告書』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2006年（平成19年） *『埋蔵文化財調査報告書 55』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2007年（平成20年） *『長塚古墳第3次発掘調査現地説明会資料』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館、2009年（平成22年） </small>
==アクセス== *[[名鉄瀬戸線]] - [[喜多山駅 (愛知県)|喜多山駅]]から徒歩7分 *[[名古屋市営バス]] -「喜多山」バス停から徒歩8分
==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html 名古屋を歩こう - 守山区 小幡] * [[国土地理院]] [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 地図・空中写真閲覧サービス] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595-39] - 1948年7月の米軍による[[空中写真]]、上端近くの中央左側に二重周濠があった当時の古墳が見られる。
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはたなかつかこふん}} [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=小幡長塚古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション=小幡長塚古墳 {{国土航空写真}} |所在地=[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]小幡4丁目地内 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E|}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=墳長約81m、前方部幅約42m、後円部径約38m |築造年代=6世紀前半 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=須恵器、形象埴輪 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }}

* 須恵器（高坏、甕） * 円筒埴輪 * 朝顔型埴輪 * 人物埴輪（腕・手の破片） * 家型埴輪 * 動物埴輪（水鳥など）
== 参考文献 == * 『長塚古墳発掘調査報告書』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2006年（平成19年） * 『埋蔵文化財調査報告書 55』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2007年（平成20年） * 『長塚古墳第3次発掘調査現地説明会資料』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館、2009年（平成22年）

revision.comment: Created page with "https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E5%B0%8F%E5%B9%A1%E9%95%B7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3"
revision.text: https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E5%B0%8F%E5%B9%A1%E9%95%B7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3

{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |Image={{ill|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|de|Datei:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|ja|ファイル:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|lt=250px|lt_ja=250px|lt_de=250px}} |Image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真}} |Location={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|de|Aichi-Präfektur|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya City|de|Stadt Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama Ward|de|Bezirk Moriyama|ja|守山区}} Obata 4‑chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole‑shaped tumulus (Zenpō‑kōen‑fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}
'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|de|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|de|Aichi-Präfektur|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya City|de|Stadt Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama Ward|de|Bezirk Moriyama|ja|守山区}}. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a {{ill|Keyhole‑shaped tumulus|de|Schlüssellochgrabhügel|ja|前方後円墳}}. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|de|Kaiserlich Japanische Armee|ja|大日本帝国陸軍|lt=Army|lt_ja=陸軍|lt_de=Armee}}, and traces of {{ill|trench|de|Schützengraben|ja|塹壕}} dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し}}. No {{ill|excavation survey|de|Ausgrabungsuntersuchung|ja|発掘調査}} has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. {{ill|fukiishi|de|Bedeckungssteine|ja|葺石}} have not been confirmed.
== Excavated Items == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the {{ill|Type 38 infantry rifle|de|Type 38 Infanteriegewehr|ja|三八式歩兵銃}} have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|de|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|lt=Meitetsu|lt_ja=名鉄|lt_de=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Seto Line|de|Seto‑Linie|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|lt=Seto Line|lt_ja=瀬戸線|lt_de=Seto‑Linie}} – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|de|Kitayama Bahnhof (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅 (愛知県)|lt=Kitayama Station|lt_ja=喜多山駅|lt_de=Kitayama Bahnhof}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|de|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|de|Geoinformationsbehörde Japan|ja|国土地理院}} [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E5%B9%A1%E9%95%B7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |Image={{ill|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|de|Datei:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|ja|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|lt=250px|lt_ja=250px|lt_de=250px}} |Image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真}} |Location={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|de|Aichi-Präfektur|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya City|de|Stadt Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama Ward|de|Bezirk Moriyama|ja|守山区}} Obata 4‑chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole‑shaped tumulus (Zenpō‑kōen‑fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}

In addition, bullets believed to be from the {{ill|Type 38 infantry rifle|de|Type 38 Infanteriegewehr|ja|三八式歩兵銃}} have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|de|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|lt=Meitetsu|lt_ja=名鉄|lt_de=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Seto Line|de|Seto‑Linie|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|lt=Seto Line|lt_ja=瀬戸線|lt_de=Seto‑Linie}} – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|de|Kitayama Bahnhof (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅 (愛知県)|lt=Kitayama Station|lt_ja=喜多山駅|lt_de=Kitayama Bahnhof}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|de|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|de|Geoinformationsbehörde Japan|ja|国土地理院}} [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E5%B9%A1%E9%95%B7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |Image={{ill|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|de|Datei:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|ja|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|lt=250px|lt_ja=250px|lt_de=250px}} |Image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真}} |Location={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|de|Aichi-Präfektur|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya City|de|Stadt Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama Ward|de|Bezirk Moriyama|ja|守山区}} Obata 4‑chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole‑shaped tumulus (Zenpō‑kōen‑fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}
'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|de|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|de|Aichi-Präfektur|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya City|de|Stadt Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama Ward|de|Bezirk Moriyama|ja|守山区}}. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a {{ill|Keyhole‑shaped tumulus|de|Schlüssellochgrabhügel|ja|前方後円墳}}. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|de|Kaiserlich Japanische Armee|ja|大日本帝国陸軍|lt=Army|lt_ja=陸軍|lt_de=Armee}}, and traces of {{ill|trench|de|Schützengraben|ja|塹壕}} dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し}}. No {{ill|excavation survey|de|Ausgrabungsuntersuchung|ja|発掘調査}} has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. {{ill|fukiishi|de|Bedeckungssteine|ja|葺石}} have not been confirmed.
== Excavated Items == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the {{ill|Type 38 infantry rifle|de|Type 38 Infanteriegewehr|ja|三八式歩兵銃}} have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|de|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|lt=Meitetsu|lt_ja=名鉄|lt_de=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Seto Line|de|Seto‑Linie|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|lt=Seto Line|lt_ja=瀬戸線|lt_de=Seto‑Linie}} – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|de|Kitayama Bahnhof (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅 (愛知県)|lt=Kitayama Station|lt_ja=喜多山駅|lt_de=Kitayama Bahnhof}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|de|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|de|Geoinformationsbehörde Japan|ja|国土地理院}} [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595] [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E5%B9%A1%E9%95%B7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|de|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|de|Aichi-Präfektur|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Nagoya City|de|Stadt Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}} {{ill|Moriyama Ward|de|Bezirk Moriyama|ja|守山区}}. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a {{ill|Keyhole‑shaped tumulus|de|Schlüssellochgrabhügel|ja|前方後円墳}}. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|de|Kaiserlich Japanische Armee|ja|大日本帝国陸軍|lt=Army|lt_ja=陸軍|lt_de=Armee}}, and traces of {{ill|trench|de|Schützengraben|ja|塹壕}} dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し}}. No {{ill|excavation survey|de|Ausgrabungsuntersuchung|ja|発掘調査}} has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. {{ill|fukiishi|de|Bedeckungssteine|ja|葺石}} have not been confirmed.
== Excavated Items == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the {{ill|Type 38 infantry rifle|de|Type 38 Infanteriegewehr|ja|三八式歩兵銃}} have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|de|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|lt=Meitetsu|lt_ja=名鉄|lt_de=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Seto Line|de|Seto‑Linie|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|lt=Seto Line|lt_ja=瀬戸線|lt_de=Seto‑Linie}} – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|de|Kitayama Bahnhof (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅 (愛知県)|lt=Kitayama Station|lt_ja=喜多山駅|lt_de=Kitayama Bahnhof}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|de|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス}} – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|de|Geoinformationsbehörde Japan|ja|国土地理院}} [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595]

== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=draft}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=draft}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|de|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|lt=Meitetsu|lt_ja=名鉄|lt_de=Meitetsu|12=draft}} {{ill|Seto Line|de|Seto‑Linie|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|lt=Seto Line|lt_ja=瀬戸線|lt_de=Seto‑Linie|12=draft}} – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|de|Kitayama Bahnhof (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅 (愛知県)|lt=Kitayama Station|lt_ja=喜多山駅|lt_de=Kitayama Bahnhof|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|de|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス|12=draft}} – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|de|Geoinformationsbehörde Japan|ja|国土地理院|12=draft}} [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595]
{{draft categories| [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E5%B0%8F%E5%B9%A1%E9%95%B7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[sn:Obata Nagatsuka Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:qq]]" to ""
revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |image={{ill|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|de|Datei:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|ja|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|lt=250px|lt_ja=250px|lt_de=250px|12=draft}} |image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}} |Location={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|de|Aichi-Präfektur|ja|愛知県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya City|de|Stadt Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}} {{ill|Moriyama Ward|de|Bezirk Moriyama|ja|守山区|12=draft}} Obata 4‑chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole‑shaped tumulus (Zenpō‑kōen‑fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}
'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|de|Kofun|ja|古墳|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|de|Aichi-Präfektur|ja|愛知県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nagoya City|de|Stadt Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}} {{ill|Moriyama Ward|de|Bezirk Moriyama|ja|守山区|12=draft}}. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a {{ill|Keyhole‑shaped tumulus|de|Schlüssellochgrabhügel|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft}}. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|de|Kaiserlich Japanische Armee|ja|大日本帝国陸軍|lt=Army|lt_ja=陸軍|lt_de=Armee|12=draft}}, and traces of {{ill|trench|de|Schützengraben|ja|塹壕|12=draft}} dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|12=draft}}. No {{ill|excavation survey|de|Ausgrabungsuntersuchung|ja|発掘調査|12=draft}} has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. {{ill|fukiishi|de|Bedeckungssteine|ja|葺石|12=draft}} have not been confirmed.
== Excavated Items == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the {{ill|Type 38 infantry rifle|de|Type 38 Infanteriegewehr|ja|三八式歩兵銃|12=draft}} have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|12=draft}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=draft}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=draft}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|de|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|lt=Meitetsu|lt_ja=名鉄|lt_de=Meitetsu|12=draft}} {{ill|Seto Line|de|Seto‑Linie|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|lt=Seto Line|lt_ja=瀬戸線|lt_de=Seto‑Linie|12=draft}} – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|de|Kitayama Bahnhof (Aichi)|ja|喜多山駅 (愛知県)|lt=Kitayama Station|lt_ja=喜多山駅|lt_de=Kitayama Bahnhof|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nagoya City Bus|de|Nagoya City Bus|ja|名古屋市営バス|12=draft}} – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|de|Geoinformationsbehörde Japan|ja|国土地理院|12=draft}} [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595]
{{draft categories|

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=小幡長塚古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション=小幡長塚古墳 {{国土航空写真}} |所在地=[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]小幡4丁目地内 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E|}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=墳長約81m、前方部幅約42m、後円部径約38m |築造年代=6世紀前半 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=須恵器、形象埴輪 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }}
'''小幡長塚古墳'''（おばたながつかこふん）は[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]にある[[古墳]]。地元では単に'''長塚古墳'''と呼ぶ。
== 概要 == 小幡ヶ原周辺に点在する古墳の一つで[[前方後円墳]]。周辺は明治時代後半から[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]]の小幡ヶ原射撃場として使用され、墳丘には演習の際に掘られた[[塹壕]]の痕跡が現在も残る。かつて墳丘は二重周濠に囲まれていたが、戦後の開発において周堤は取り壊され、周濠は全て埋め立てられた。現在では墳丘の側まで住宅が密集している。なお、墳丘自体は名古屋市の管理する緑地「喜多山公園」として保存されている。
[[名古屋市教育委員会]]による[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）以降の墳丘・周濠の調査によって、[[須恵器]]や[[埴輪]]の破片が多数見つかっており、墳丘や出土品の形態などから[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたと考えられている。
なお、地元住民によれば、かつては複数の[[陪塚]]があったことからこの周辺は'''七塚'''と呼ばれていたともいうが、現存せず記録も残されていないため塚の規模や数については不明である。
== 規模と構造 == 主軸は北西30.5°で、墳丘長約81メートル、前方部の幅約42メートル（推定）、高さが5.5メートル、後円部径約38メートル（推定）、高さが6.5メートル。先に記したように墳丘の近くまで住宅があるため内濠を含めた墳裾部の詳細な調査は行なわれておらず、墳丘の規模については必ずしも確定した数値ではない。近年の調査では墳丘長約74メートル、前方部幅約48メートル、後円部径約42メートルの数値も挙げられている。また、2段築造であったと推定されており、くびれ部北東側に[[造り出し]]の痕跡らしきものがみられる。墳丘の[[発掘調査]]は行なわれておらず内部主体は不明。[[葺石]]は確認されていない。
[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）の愛知県の測量記録によれば墳丘は盾型の二重周濠に囲まれており、外堤の高さは180センチメートルほどであった。[[2006年]]（平成18年）の調査で北側の外堤に方形張出部が存在した可能性が指摘されている。また、[[愛知県道59号名古屋中環状線]]（茶臼山交差点〜小幡ヶ原交差点間）建設に伴う[[2008年]]（平成20年）度の調査では前方部北側の中堤の角が検出され、先の発掘調査の結果と重ね合わせると、前方部外濠の幅は18〜19メートルに及び、周濠を含めた古墳の全長135〜140メートル、外濠前面幅約125メートルと推定されている。
== 出土品 == くびれ部に近い外濠の中堤近辺から多数が出土しているがほとんどが破片で、出土状況などから後年になって意図的に破壊された可能性もあるとしている。人物埴輪の出土は名古屋市内の古墳では初。また、[[三八式歩兵銃]]のものとみられる弾丸なども出土しており、出土品は[[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]に収蔵されている。
* 須恵器（高坏、甕） * 円筒埴輪 * 朝顔型埴輪 * 人物埴輪（腕・手の破片） * 家型埴輪 * 動物埴輪（水鳥など）
== 参考文献 == * 『長塚古墳発掘調査報告書』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2006年（平成19年） * 『埋蔵文化財調査報告書 55』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館 編、名古屋市教育委員会 発行、2007年（平成20年） * 『長塚古墳第3次発掘調査現地説明会資料』- 名古屋市見晴台考古資料館、2009年（平成22年）
== アクセス == * [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄瀬戸線|瀬戸線]] - [[喜多山駅 (愛知県)|喜多山駅]]から徒歩で約7分。 * [[名古屋市営バス]] -「喜多山」バス停から徒歩で約8分。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html 名古屋を歩こう - 守山区 小幡] * [[国土地理院]] [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 地図・空中写真閲覧サービス] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595-39] - 1948年7月の米軍による[[空中写真]]、上端近くの中央左側に二重周濠があった当時の古墳が見られる。
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはたなかつかこふん}} [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[小幡長塚古墳]] to [[Obata Nagatsuka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html 名古屋を歩こう - 守山区 小幡] * [[国土地理院]] [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 地図・空中写真閲覧サービス] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595-39] - 1948年7月の米軍による[[空中写真]]、上端近くの中央左側に二重周濠があった当時の古墳が見られる。

{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |image={{ill|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|de|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|ja|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|lt=250px|lt_ja=250px|lt_de=250px|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491397}} |Location=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Obata 4‑chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole‑shaped tumulus (Zenpō‑kōen‑fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}
'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]]. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Army]], and traces of [[:en:Trench warfare|trench]] dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Tsukuridashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No [[:en:Archaeological excavation|excavation survey]] has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the [[:en:Type 38 rifle|Type 38 infantry rifle]] have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|qq=|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|qq=|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|qq=|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from [[:en:Kitayama Station (Aichi)|Kitayama Station]]. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * [[:en:Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan]] [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595]

{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |image={{ill|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|de|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|ja|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|lt=250px|lt_ja=250px|lt_de=250px|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}} |Location=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Obata 4‑chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole‑shaped tumulus (Zenpō‑kōen‑fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}
'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]]. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Army]], and traces of [[:en:Trench warfare|trench]] dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Tsukuridashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No [[:en:Archaeological excavation|excavation survey]] has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the [[:en:Type 38 rifle|Type 38 infantry rifle]] have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from [[:en:Kitayama Station (Aichi)|Kitayama Station]]. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * [[:en:Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan]] [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595]

{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |image={{ill|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|de|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|ja|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|lt=250px|lt_ja=250px|lt_de=250px|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work}} |image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}} |Location=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Obata 4‑chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole‑shaped tumulus (Zenpō‑kōen‑fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}
'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]]. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Army]], and traces of [[:en:Trench warfare|trench]] dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Tsukuridashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No [[:en:Archaeological excavation|excavation survey]] has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the [[:en:Type 38 rifle|Type 38 infantry rifle]] have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from [[:en:Kitayama Station (Aichi)|Kitayama Station]]. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * [[:en:Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan]] [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595]

{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |image=[[Category:Pages with files linked by ill]]{{ill|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|de|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|ja|File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|lt=250px|lt_ja=250px|lt_de=250px|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} |image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}} |Location=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Obata 4‑chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole‑shaped tumulus (Zenpō‑kōen‑fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}
'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]]. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Army]], and traces of [[:en:Trench warfare|trench]] dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Tsukuridashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No [[:en:Archaeological excavation|excavation survey]] has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the [[:en:Type 38 rifle|Type 38 infantry rifle]] have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from [[:en:Kitayama Station (Aichi)|Kitayama Station]]. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * [[:en:Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan]] [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595]
[[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|小幡長塚古墳|version=93790496|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]]
}}

The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. '''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]]. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Army]], and traces of [[:en:Trench warfare|trench]] dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Tsukuridashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No [[:en:Archaeological excavation|excavation survey]] has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the [[:en:Type 38 rifle|Type 38 infantry rifle]] have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)

{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |image=[[File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|thumb]] |image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}} |Location=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Obata 4‑chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole‑shaped tumulus (Zenpō‑kōen‑fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}
'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]]. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Army]], and traces of [[:en:Trench warfare|trench]] dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Tsukuridashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No [[:en:Archaeological excavation|excavation survey]] has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the [[:en:Type 38 rifle|Type 38 infantry rifle]] have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from [[:en:Kitayama Station (Aichi)|Kitayama Station]]. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * [[:en:Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan]] [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595]
