'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]]. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Army]], and traces of [[:en:Trench warfare|trench]] dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Tsukuridashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No [[:en:Archaeological excavation|excavation survey]] has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the [[:en:Type 38 rifle|Type 38 infantry rifle]] have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小幡長塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小幡長塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小幡長塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.207072|N|136.983303|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11460707]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |image=[[File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|thumb]] |image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}} |Location=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] Obata 4‑chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole‑shaped tumulus (Zenpō‑kōen‑fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}
'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya City]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]]. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Army]], and traces of [[:en:Trench warfare|trench]] dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Tsukuridashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No [[:en:Archaeological excavation|excavation survey]] has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the [[:en:Type 38 rifle|Type 38 infantry rifle]] have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Meitetsu]] [[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Seto Line]] – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from [[:en:Kitayama Station (Aichi)|Kitayama Station]]. * [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Bus|Nagoya City Bus]] – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * [[:en:Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan]] [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595]

{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |image=[[File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|thumb]] |image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}} |Location={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya City}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward}} Obata 4‑chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole‑shaped tumulus (Zenpō‑kōen‑fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}
'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya City}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward}}. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Army}}, and traces of {{ill|Trench warfare|en|Trench warfare|WD=Q241059|lt=trench}} dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Tsukuridashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No {{ill|Archaeological excavation|en|Archaeological excavation|WD=Q959782|lt=excavation survey}} has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the {{ill|Type 38 rifle|en|Type 38 rifle|WD=Q663662|lt=Type 38 infantry rifle}} have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|en|Meitetsu Seto Line|WD=Q6810422|lt=Seto Line}} – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|en|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|WD=Q6417930|lt=Kitayama Station}}. * {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Bus|en|Nagoya Municipal Bus|WD=Q11414982|lt=Nagoya City Bus}} – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|en|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|WD=Q2986578|lt=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}} [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595] {{translated page|ja|小幡長塚古墳|version=93790496|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |image=[[File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|thumb]] |image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}} |Location={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya City}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward}} Obata 4‑chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole‑shaped tumulus (Zenpō‑kōen‑fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}
'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya City}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward}}. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Army}}, and traces of {{ill|Trench warfare|en|Trench warfare|WD=Q241059|lt=trench}} dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Tsukuridashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No {{ill|Archaeological excavation|en|Archaeological excavation|WD=Q959782|lt=excavation survey}} has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the {{ill|Type 38 rifle|en|Type 38 rifle|WD=Q663662|lt=Type 38 infantry rifle}} have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high‑fired, jar‑shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory‑shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House‑shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On‑site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|en|Meitetsu Seto Line|WD=Q6810422|lt=Seto Line}} – Approximately a 7‑minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|en|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|WD=Q6417930|lt=Kitayama Station}}. * {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Bus|en|Nagoya Municipal Bus|WD=Q11414982|lt=Nagoya City Bus}} – Approximately an 8‑minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|en|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|WD=Q2986578|lt=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}} [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595] {{translated page|ja|小幡長塚古墳|version=93790496|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Army|1=Imperial Japanese Army}}, and traces of {{ill|Trench warfare|en|Trench warfare|WD=Q241059|lt=trench|1=Trench warfare}} dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Platform-style projection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No {{ill|Archaeological excavation|en|Archaeological excavation|WD=Q959782|lt=excavation survey|1=Archaeological excavation}} has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the {{ill|Type 38 rifle|en|Type 38 rifle|WD=Q663662|lt=Type 38 infantry rifle|1=Type 38 rifle}} have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high-fired, jar-shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory-shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House-shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On-site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|en|Meitetsu Seto Line|WD=Q6810422|lt=Seto Line|1=Meitetsu Seto Line}} – Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|en|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|WD=Q6417930|lt=Kitayama Station|1=Kitayama Station (Aichi)}}. * {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Bus|en|Nagoya Municipal Bus|WD=Q11414982|lt=Nagoya City Bus|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} – Approximately an 8-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}

As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Platform-style projection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No {{ill|Archaeological excavation|en|Archaeological excavation|WD=Q959782|lt=excavation survey|1=Archaeological excavation}} has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the {{ill|Type 38 rifle|en|Type 38 rifle|WD=Q663662|lt=Type 38 infantry rifle|1=Type 38 rifle}} have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high-fired, jar-shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory-shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House-shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On-site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|en|Meitetsu Seto Line|WD=Q6810422|lt=Seto Line|1=Meitetsu Seto Line}} – Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|en|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|WD=Q6417930|lt=Kitayama Station|1=Kitayama Station (Aichi)}}. * {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Bus|en|Nagoya Municipal Bus|WD=Q11414982|lt=Nagoya City Bus|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} – Approximately an 8-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Obata Nagatsuka kofun}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/moriyama7.html Walk Nagoya – Moriyama Ward: Obata] * {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|en|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|WD=Q2986578|lt=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}} [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/maplibSearch.do#1 Map and Aerial Photograph Viewing Service] ** [https://mapps.gsi.go.jp/contentsImage.do?specificationId=202781&dispType=1 USA-R1595] {{translated page|ja|小幡長塚古墳|version=93790496|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun (小幡長塚古墳) |image=[[File:Obata-Nagatsuka-kofun01.jpg|thumb]] |image caption=Obata Nagatsuka Kofun {{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|de|Nationale Luftaufnahme|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}} |Location={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya City|1=Nagoya}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}} Obata 4-chome area |Coordinates={{WikiCoord2DMS|35|12|25.46|N|136|58|59.89|E}} |Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (Zenpō-kōen-fun, 前方後円墳) |Scale=Tumulus length approximately 81 m, front section width approximately 42 m, rear circular section diameter approximately 38 m |Date of construction=Early 6th century |Burial facility= |Buried individual= |Excavated items=Sue ware, figurative haniwa |Historic Site designation= |Notes= }}
'''Obata Nagatsuka Kofun''' (おばたながつかこふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya City|1=Nagoya}} {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}. Locally, it is simply known as '''Nagatsuka Kofun'''.
== Overview == It is one of the kofun scattered around the Obatagahara area and is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}. The surrounding area was used from the late Meiji period onward as the Obatagahara firing range by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Army|1=Imperial Japanese Army}}, and traces of {{ill|Trench warfare|en|Trench warfare|WD=Q241059|lt=trench|1=Trench warfare}} dug during military exercises still remain on the mound. Although the tumulus was once surrounded by a double moat, postwar development led to the demolition of the outer embankment and the complete infilling of the moats. Nowadays, houses are built densely right up to the side of the mound. The mound itself is preserved as Kitayama Park, a green space managed by Nagoya City.
== Scale and Structure == The main axis is oriented at 30.5° northwest, with the tumulus mound measuring approximately 81 meters in length, an estimated front section width of about 42 meters with a height of 5.5 meters, and an estimated rear circular section diameter of about 38 meters with a height of 6.5 meters. As mentioned earlier, because houses have been built close to the mound, a detailed survey of its lower portion (including the inner moat) has not been carried out, and the dimensions are not definitively established. Recent surveys have yielded figures of approximately 74 meters in mound length, 48 meters for the front section width, and 42 meters for the rear circular section diameter. It is also estimated to have been built in two tiers, and on the northeast side of the neck region there appears to be evidence of a {{ill|projection|de|Ausladung|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/projection|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出|1=Platform-style projection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}. No {{ill|Archaeological excavation|en|Archaeological excavation|WD=Q959782|lt=excavation survey|1=Archaeological excavation}} has been conducted on the mound, so its internal structure remains unknown. {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} have not been confirmed.
== burial goods == Many items have been excavated from near the outer embankment of the moat by the neck region, but most are fragments; the excavation conditions suggest that they may have been deliberately destroyed at a later date. The recovery of a figurative haniwa representing a person is a first among the kofun in Nagoya City. In addition, bullets believed to be from the {{ill|Type 38 rifle|en|Type 38 rifle|WD=Q663662|lt=Type 38 infantry rifle|1=Type 38 rifle}} have also been excavated, and these items are stored at the {{ill|Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|de|Nagoya City Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|ja|見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt=Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|lt_ja=名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|lt_de=Mishidai Archäologisches Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}.
* Sue ware (high-fired, jar-shaped) * Cylindrical haniwa * Morning glory-shaped haniwa * Figurative haniwa (fragments of arms and hands) * House-shaped haniwa * Animal haniwa (such as waterfowl)
== References == * "Nagatsuka Kofun Excavation Survey Report" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2006 (Heisei 19) * "Report on Buried Cultural Property Survey 55" – Edited by Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, published by {{ill|Nagoya City Board of Education|de|Bildungsbehörde Nagoya|ja|名古屋市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Board of Education|1=Nagoya Municipal Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415003}}, 2007 (Heisei 20) * "Nagatsuka Kofun Third Excavation Survey On-site Briefing Materials" – Nagoya City Mishidai Archaeological Museum, 2009 (Heisei 22)
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|en|Meitetsu Seto Line|WD=Q6810422|lt=Seto Line|1=Meitetsu Seto Line}} – Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|en|Kitayama Station (Aichi)|WD=Q6417930|lt=Kitayama Station|1=Kitayama Station (Aichi)}}. * {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Bus|en|Nagoya Municipal Bus|WD=Q11414982|lt=Nagoya City Bus|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} – Approximately an 8-minute walk from the "Kitayama" bus stop.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Kofun|de|Liste der japanischen Kofun|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Kofun|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
